nodes.bodyText:
Pull request overview
This PR updates Maester’s CI publishing and validation flow to build/publish from the optimized ./module artifact, and refreshes contributor documentation to reflect the new build-from-source workflow.
Changes:

Publish workflows now build ./module via ./build/Build-MaesterModule.ps1, validate it, then publish/zip from the built output.
Adds ./build/Test-MaesterModuleOutput.ps1 for fail-fast post-build validation (expected files, version stamp, import, command count, help).
Updates contributor docs and README to describe the new build artifact and development guidance; removes the old test-copy build helper.

Reviewed changes
Copilot reviewed 10 out of 10 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

Show a summary per file



File
Description




website/docs/contributing.md
Documents the new build output (./module) and adds style/org guidance.


README.md
Adds “Building from source” instructions referencing the build script and ./module.


powershell/public/core/Install-MaesterTests.ps1
Updates dev note to use Build-MaesterModule.ps1 instead of the removed copy script.


build/Test-MaesterModuleOutput.ps1
New post-build validation script used by CI workflows.


build/Copy-MaesterTestsToPSModule.ps1
Removes obsolete test-copy build helper.


.github/workflows/publish-module.yaml
Builds + validates ./module, stages to publish/Maester, publishes + zips from built artifact.


.github/workflows/publish-module-preview.yaml
Same as publish-module, plus artifacts now come from ./module.


.github/workflows/publish-module-manualversionupdate.yaml
Same as publish-module, using built + validated ./module.


.github/workflows/build-validation.yaml
Adds build/** to path filter and runs build + output validation across OS matrix.


.github/CONTRIBUTING.md
Adds a short “Build Process” section pointing to the website contributing guide.





💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

Actionable comments posted: 3

🧹 Nitpick comments (2)

.github/workflows/build-validation.yaml (2)
29-30: 🔒 Security & Privacy | 🔵 Trivial | 💤 Low value
Consider hardening actions/checkout with persist-credentials: false.
zizmor flags this checkout step for credential persistence (artipacked). Newer checkout versions (v6+) store persisted credentials outside .git/config, which reduces the classic exfiltration-via-artifact vector, but explicitly disabling persistence is still a low-cost hardening default here since these jobs don't push/fetch with the token.

🔒 Suggested hardening
       - uses: actions/checkout@9c091bb21b7c1c1d1991bb908d89e4e9dddfe3e0 # v7.0.0
+        with:
+          persist-credentials: false


🤖 Prompt for AI Agents
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In @.github/workflows/build-validation.yaml around lines 29 - 30, The checkout
step in the workflow is leaving token credentials persisted by default, so
harden the `actions/checkout` usage by explicitly setting `persist-credentials:
false` on the `checkout` step. Update the workflow job that uses
`actions/checkout` in `build-validation.yaml`, keeping the existing pinned
action reference, and ensure the following `dorny/paths-filter` step still runs
unchanged.



Source: Linters/SAST tools

1-1: 📐 Maintainability & Code Quality | 🔵 Trivial | 💤 Low value
Normalize line endings.
yamllint flags a line-ending mismatch on this file (expected \n). Likely CRLF vs LF; worth normalizing via .gitattributes or editor config to keep lint clean.

🤖 Prompt for AI Agents
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In @.github/workflows/build-validation.yaml at line 1, The build-validation
workflow file has inconsistent line endings that trigger yamllint; normalize it
to LF throughout and prevent recurrence by enforcing the same end-of-line
setting in repository config. Update the workflow content itself and, if needed,
add or adjust the repo-level line-ending policy so this file and similar YAML
files stay on consistent Unix line endings.



Source: Linters/SAST tools



🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

Inline comments:
In @.github/workflows/publish-module.yaml:
- Around line 54-85: The build/validate/publish sequence in the publish workflow
is duplicated across multiple workflow files, so extract the shared steps into a
reusable composite action or workflow helper and have this workflow call it
instead. Move the repeated logic around Build-MaesterModule.ps1,
Test-MaesterModuleOutput.ps1, Publish-Module, and Compress-Archive into a shared
action (for example, a build-and-publish module action), and parameterize values
like expected-version and prerelease-specific behavior so the publish workflows
stay thin and consistent.
- Around line 61-64: The dependency installation step in the publish workflow is
pulling unpinned PowerShell modules and bypassing publisher checks, which can
run untrusted code in the same job as the Gallery secret. Update the
Install-Module call in the install-dependencies step to use pinned or minimum
versions for Pester and Microsoft.Graph.Authentication and remove
-SkipPublisherCheck and other broad bypasses. If possible, move this dependency
setup out of the job that later uses secrets.PS_GALLERY_KEY so the install step
cannot access the publish credential. Use the existing install-dependencies step
and the publish step as the locations to adjust.

In `@website/docs/contributing.md`:
- Around line 53-58: The new “File organization” guidance in the docs conflicts
with the older “Pester Tests” text, so update the existing Pester Tests section
to match the current test layout. In the contributing doc, revise the wording
around the “Pester Tests” heading so it references the top-level ./tests
location used by the suite and the related build/workflow symbols like
tests/maester-config.json and Build-MaesterModule.ps1, keeping the terminology
consistent with the new “File organization” section.

---

Nitpick comments:
In @.github/workflows/build-validation.yaml:
- Around line 29-30: The checkout step in the workflow is leaving token
credentials persisted by default, so harden the `actions/checkout` usage by
explicitly setting `persist-credentials: false` on the `checkout` step. Update
the workflow job that uses `actions/checkout` in `build-validation.yaml`,
keeping the existing pinned action reference, and ensure the following
`dorny/paths-filter` step still runs unchanged.
- Line 1: The build-validation workflow file has inconsistent line endings that
trigger yamllint; normalize it to LF throughout and prevent recurrence by
enforcing the same end-of-line setting in repository config. Update the workflow
content itself and, if needed, add or adjust the repo-level line-ending policy
so this file and similar YAML files stay on consistent Unix line endings.



🪄 Autofix (Beta)
Fix all unresolved CodeRabbit comments on this PR:

  Push a commit to this branch (recommended)
  Create a new PR with the fixes




ℹ️ Review info

⚙️ Run configuration
Configuration used: Path: .coderabbit.yaml
Review profile: CHILL
Plan: Pro Plus
Run ID: 5ac91f07-592d-4cc7-8e4b-8fd2fb090cad


📥 Commits
Reviewing files that changed from the base of the PR and between 0cfb82f and 6805ae6.


📒 Files selected for processing (10)

.github/CONTRIBUTING.md
.github/workflows/build-validation.yaml
.github/workflows/publish-module-manualversionupdate.yaml
.github/workflows/publish-module-preview.yaml
.github/workflows/publish-module.yaml
README.md
build/Copy-MaesterTestsToPSModule.ps1
build/Test-MaesterModuleOutput.ps1
powershell/public/core/Install-MaesterTests.ps1
website/docs/contributing.md



💤 Files with no reviewable changes (1)

build/Copy-MaesterTestsToPSModule.ps1

The new dev guide distinguishes bundled test suites in ./tests, but the “Pester Tests” bullets below use absolute-style paths (/powershell/tests) and don’t clarify these are the repository/CI Pester tests. This is likely to confuse contributors on where to run which tests; please switch to repo-relative paths and clarify the intent.

This code-style bullet says “end every file with a single newline”, but the repo’s documented standard (and the optimization plan referenced in this PR) calls for a blank line at EOF. “Single newline” is ambiguous and may lead to inconsistent formatting.

The README now links to the contributing guide on maester.dev, but the “Contributing” section immediately below still points to preview.maester.dev. This inconsistency can send users to different versions of the docs; please align the links to the same canonical site.

📐 Maintainability & Code Quality | 🟠 Major | ⚡ Quick win
Build/validate/publish block is duplicated verbatim across all three publish workflows.
This exact ~30-line sequence (build → install deps → validate → stage → publish → zip) is repeated in publish-module.yaml, publish-module-preview.yaml, and publish-module-manualversionupdate.yaml. The linked issue (#1566) objectives explicitly call for workflows to "avoid duplicated build logic," which this duplication contradicts.
Consider extracting this into a composite action (e.g. .github/actions/build-and-publish-module) parameterized by expected-version, prerelease, etc., and call it from all three workflows.

🧰 Tools

🪛 zizmor (1.26.1)
[info] 69-69: code injection via template expansion (template-injection): may expand into attacker-controllable code
(template-injection)



🤖 Prompt for AI Agents
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In @.github/workflows/publish-module.yaml around lines 54 - 85, The
build/validate/publish sequence in the publish workflow is duplicated across
multiple workflow files, so extract the shared steps into a reusable composite
action or workflow helper and have this workflow call it instead. Move the
repeated logic around Build-MaesterModule.ps1, Test-MaesterModuleOutput.ps1,
Publish-Module, and Compress-Archive into a shared action (for example, a
build-and-publish module action), and parameterize values like expected-version
and prerelease-specific behavior so the publish workflows stay thin and
consistent.

🔒 Security & Privacy | 🟠 Major | ⚡ Quick win
Unpinned, publisher-check-bypassed module install runs in the same job as the Gallery publish secret.
Install-Module Pester, Microsoft.Graph.Authentication -Force -SkipPublisherCheck ... installs unpinned versions and explicitly skips publisher verification, immediately before a step that uses secrets.PS_GALLERY_KEY in the same job (Line 79). If either package is compromised or typosquatted, its module init code could run before/alongside the publish step and access the Gallery API key in the job environment.
Recommend pinning exact/minimum versions with -RequiredVersion/-MinimumVersion and dropping -SkipPublisherCheck, or moving dependency installation to a job/step that has no access to PS_GALLERY_KEY.

🤖 Prompt for AI Agents
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In @.github/workflows/publish-module.yaml around lines 61 - 64, The dependency
installation step in the publish workflow is pulling unpinned PowerShell modules
and bypassing publisher checks, which can run untrusted code in the same job as
the Gallery secret. Update the Install-Module call in the install-dependencies
step to use pinned or minimum versions for Pester and
Microsoft.Graph.Authentication and remove -SkipPublisherCheck and other broad
bypasses. If possible, move this dependency setup out of the job that later uses
secrets.PS_GALLERY_KEY so the install step cannot access the publish credential.
Use the existing install-dependencies step and the publish step as the locations
to adjust.

📐 Maintainability & Code Quality | 🟡 Minor | ⚡ Quick win
New "File organization" section conflicts with the existing "Pester Tests" section below.
Line 57 states Pester test suite files live in ./tests/<SuiteName>, but the unchanged "Pester Tests" section (Line 61) still says Tests for the Maester module are at /powershell/tests. Other evidence in this PR (e.g. workflows referencing tests/maester-config.json, Build-MaesterModule.ps1 copying $TestsRoot into the built output) confirms tests actually live at top-level ./tests, so Line 61 is stale and should be updated to match the new section for consistency.

📝 Proposed fix for Line 61
-- Tests for the Maester module are at /powershell/tests
+- Tests for the Maester module are at ./tests



📝 Committable suggestion

‼️ IMPORTANT
Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

  
    
      
        Suggested change
      
    
    
      
          
            
            ### File organization
          
          
            
            
          
          
            
            - Public (exported) functions go in `./powershell/public`. One function per file, and the filename must match the function name exactly.
          
          
            
            - Internal helper functions go in `./powershell/internal`. These are never exported.
          
          
            
            - Pester test suite files go in `./tests/<SuiteName>`.
          
          
            
             Tests for the Maester module are at ./tests
          
      
    
    
  




🤖 Prompt for AI Agents
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@website/docs/contributing.md` around lines 53 - 58, The new “File
organization” guidance in the docs conflicts with the older “Pester Tests” text,
so update the existing Pester Tests section to match the current test layout. In
the contributing doc, revise the wording around the “Pester Tests” heading so it
references the top-level ./tests location used by the suite and the related
build/workflow symbols like tests/maester-config.json and
Build-MaesterModule.ps1, keeping the terminology consistent with the new “File
organization” section.
